title: SPF / DKIM / DMARC
html:
Stub. The three DNS-layer authentication standards that prove an email is from who it claims to be from:

  • SPF — list of IPs allowed to send for the domain. Should end with -all (hard fail) for strictness.
  • DKIM — cryptographic signature on the message headers, verified against a public key in DNS.
  • DMARC — policy telling receivers what to do when SPF/DKIM fail (none/quarantine/reject).

Failing any of these tanks email deliverability. First mentioned in 2025 09 24 unspam deliverability test.
